topics: 👁️ סמא את עיני — consent to bodily damage with 'on condition not to be liable'
🤔 R' Lazar vs. Reish Laqish — does 'yes' really mean 'no'?
💰 Coerced by Gentiles: handing over another's money
🪵 גזל עצים ועשאן כלים — change in the object and time of payment
🧼 Is bleaching wool considered a שינוי?

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Consent, Coercion, and the Robbed Object
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf closes Chapter 8 with the laws of consenting to one's own injury (סמא את עיני) and being coerced by Gentiles to hand over a fellow's money. It then opens Chapter 9: the foundational principle that a robber pays the value of the object at the time of the gezeilah, even if it has since been transformed into utensils or garments. The amoraim debate whether bleaching wool counts as a שינוי that locks in the gazlan's payment.
overview.hebrewSource: הָאוֹמֵר סַמֵּא אֵת עֵינִי קְטַע אֶת יָדִי שַׁבֵּר אֶת רַגְלִי חַיָיב עַל מְנָת לִפְטוֹר חַיָיב

slides.title: 👁️ Body vs. Property — Consent Cuts Differently
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah distinguishes between damage to the body and damage to property.

boxes.title: 🩸 'Blind my eye' (גופו)
items: Even עַל מְנָת לִפְטוֹר → still liable
A person cannot waive bodily damage

boxes.title: 👥 To a third party
items: Even on condition to be exempt → liable
Body and money — no waiver works

callout.label: Key move:
callout.text: Consent shifts liability for property but not for body. And no one can grant exemption on someone else's behalf.

slides.title: 💬 Does 'Yes' Mean 'Yes' or 'No'?
slides.subtitle: R' Lazar and Reish Laqish split on how to read the Mishnah's 'on condition'.

lines.speaker: RL
lines.text: R' Lazar: The Mishnah is talking about a 'yes' that really means 'no' (בָּהֵן שֶׁהוּא כְלָאו). But a sincere 'yes' that means 'yes' — exempt.

lines.speaker: RŠ
lines.text: Reish Laqish: The Mishnah is the genuine 'yes' (בָּהֵן שֶׁהוּא כְהֵין). But a 'yes' that means 'no' — even more so, liable.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: Implication: R' Lazar is concerned with פְּגַם מִשְׁפָּחָה — the family's loss of standing. Reish Laqish implies one assesses bushet for relatives.

callout.label: Why it matters:
callout.text: The dispute decides whether a victim's permission can ever truly exempt the מזיק. R' Lazar reads consent narrowly; Reish Laqish reads it more literally.

slides.title: 🛡️ Coerced by Gentiles — When Are You Liable?
slides.subtitle: A baraita on a Jew forced to give over another's money.

steps.content: 1️⃣ Gentiles forcibly take a fellow's money in his presence → פָּטוּר.
2️⃣ He actively handed it over (נָטַל וְנָתַן לָהֶם) → חַיָיב.
3️⃣ R' Yose qualifies: this is when they demanded 'money' generically (מָמוֹן סְתָם).
4️⃣ But if they specified a particular person's money (מָמוֹן פְּלוֹנִי) — even if he handed it over, exempt.
5️⃣ Logic: when they targeted that specific money, his hand-over did not change the outcome.

callout.label: Practical reading:
callout.text: Liability tracks how much added harm the actor caused. Specificity by the coercer breaks the chain of responsibility.

slides.title: 🪵 Mishnah 9:1 — The Robber Pays at Time of Theft
slides.subtitle: When the gazlan transforms the object, what does he pay?

nodes.content:
גָּזַל עֵצִים וַעֲשָׂאָן כֵּלִים
(robbed wood, made utensils)

מְשַׁלֵּם כְּשָׁעַת הַגְּזֵילָה
Pays the value at the time of the robbery

columns.label: Became pregnant with him → calved
columns.content: Pays value at time of gezeilah

nodes.content: זֶה הַכְּלָל כָּל הַגַּזְלָנִין מְשַׁלְּמִין כְּשָׁעַת הַגְּזֵילָה

callout.label: The principle:
callout.text: Once שינוי kicks in, the original owner can no longer demand the transformed object. The gazlan pays the snapshot price from the moment of theft.

slides.title: 🧼 Is Bleaching a שינוי?
slides.subtitle: The Yerushalmi reconciles two contradictory rulings.
rows: He turned it into צוּפִים (wool flakes)
Bleaching alone does not change ownership
Either way: קַל הוּא שֶׁהֵיקֵלוּ בְגַזְלָן
It is a leniency for the robber to pay כשעת הגזילה
Yet again — leniency for the gazlan

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The amoraim navigate between פְּגָם and שִׁינּוּי. R' Yudan steps back and says the entire framework is a leniency — protect the gazlan from ever-changing valuations by freezing the price at the gezeilah moment.

summary.principle: Consent waives property damage but not bodily harm; coercion liability depends on specificity; and the robber always pays the value at the time of the theft.

flowSteps.content: Body vs. property → only property accepts עַל מְנָת לִפְטוֹר

🎓 Key Principle:
Liability tracks the moment of damage or theft. After that, transformation, coercion, or consent are governed by carefully calibrated rules.

quiz.q: What does the Mishnah hold about 'סמא את עיני על מנת לפטור'?
o: He is liable — bodily damage cannot be waived
Only the victim's family can sue
quiz.e: Bodily damage cannot be waived: 'על מנת לפטור חייב'. Property damage, however, can be waived.

quiz.q: How does R' Lazar read the Mishnah's 'on condition not to be liable'?
o: A 'yes' that really means 'no' (בהן שהוא כלאו)
quiz.e: R' Lazar: the Mishnah is dealing with a 'yes' which really means 'no'. Reish Laqish takes the opposite stance.

quiz.q: If Gentiles demand 'X's money specifically' and the Jew hands it over, what is his status?
o: Exempt — even if he handed it over (R' Yose)
quiz.e: R' Yose: 'אבל ממון פלוני אפילו נטל ונתן ביד פטור.' Specificity by the coercer breaks the chain of responsibility.

quiz.q: What does 'משלם כשעת הגזילה' mean in the opening Mishnah of Chapter 9?
o: He pays the current market price
He pays the value at the time of the robbery
He pays whatever the victim demands
quiz.e: The general principle: 'זה הכלל כל הגזלנין משלמין כשעת הגזילה.' Once שינוי occurs, the price is locked at the moment of theft.

quiz.q: What is R' Yudan's framing of the bleaching/שינוי dispute?
o: It is a leniency for the gazlan that he pays כשעת הגזילה
It depends on the type of wool
quiz.e: R' Yudan: 'קל הוא שהיקלו בגזלן שישלם כשעת הגזילה.' The whole framework is a calibrated leniency for the robber.
